subject: Public Health
title: Alcohol and Cancer
description: A 60-second lesson on Alcohol and Cancer.

hook.text: Alcohol causes 7 types of cancer—yet most people don't know that even moderate drinking increases cancer risk.

buildup.text: Alcohol is classified as a Group 1 carcinogen by the IARC—in the same category as tobacco and asbestos.

discovery.text: Cancers of the mouth, throat, esophagus, liver, colon, rectum, and breast are all causally linked to alcohol.

twist.text: Even one drink per day increases breast cancer risk by 7-10%—there is no safe threshold for cancer.

climax.text: Cancer warning labels on alcohol—like those on cigarettes—are being adopted by some countries to inform the public.

punchline.text: Alcohol sits beside tobacco as a proven carcinogen.

quiz.question: How many types of cancer does alcohol cause?
